articles.title: ICE Agents Are Now Patrolling U.S. Airports. Here’s What to Know.
articles.summary: The deployment comes as a battle over Department of Homeland Security funding has led to closed security checkpoints, long lines and missed flights.

articles.title: Trump administration to pay French company $1B to drop U.S. offshore wind leases
articles.summary: TotalEnergies has agreed to what's essentially a refund of its leases for projects off the coasts of North Carolina and New York, and will invest the money in fossil fuel projects instead, the Departm

articles.title: Trump Delays Energy Strikes, but Iran’s Infrastructure Is Already Battered
articles.summary: U.S. and Israeli attacks on power plants and other civilian infrastructure risk escalating the conflict across the region, and angering Iranians who oppose the government.

articles.title: Trump Administration to Pay $1 Billion to Energy Giant to Cancel Wind Farms
articles.summary: In exchange, the French company TotalEnergies would invest in oil and natural gas projects in Texas and elsewhere.

articles.title: Mapping ICE's expanding footprint, and the communities fighting back
articles.summary: Resistance in both Democratic and Republican cities points to broader unease with the direction of immigration enforcement.

articles.title: Supreme Court Appears Poised to Reject Late-Arriving Mail-In Ballots Law
articles.summary: A majority of the justices appeared skeptical of Mississippi’s mail-in ballot law in a case that could upend the way that states handle mail-in ballots throughout the country.

articles.title: Pentagon Officials Weigh Deployment of Airborne Troops for Iran War
articles.summary: The combat forces would come from a brigade of about 3,000 soldiers capable of deploying anywhere in the world within 18 hours.

articles.title: President Trump and Iranian officials have conflicting stories about ongoing talks
articles.summary: President Trump is ordering the U.S. military to hold off on striking Iranian power plants, citing "productive" talks with Iran, but Iranian officials deny there's any dialogue with the U.S.

articles.title: 8 architecture and culture groups sue Trump and the Kennedy Center board
articles.summary: The groups, which include the American Institute of Architects, are asking for compliance with historic preservation laws and to secure approval from Congress.

articles.title: In California, a Republican Sheriff Seizes Ballots, Prompting Concerns
articles.summary: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, a candidate for governor, recently took possession of more than 650,000 ballots as part of a fraud probe. Election officials say his investigation is baseless.

articles.title: What’s It Like to Be Back in Print After 20 Years? A Bit Odd.
articles.summary: Nancy Lemann published her first novel at 28. Then came “the doom.” Now she’s back in the spotlight, and not exactly comfortable with it.

articles.title: Trump Is Digging Up Washington. Can Lawsuits Stop the Bulldozers?
articles.summary: As the president develops plans to fundamentally alter the White House, the Kennedy Center and other sites, federal lawsuits are beginning to catch up.

articles.title: Supreme Court declines to review press freedom case
articles.summary: At issue was the 2017 arrest in Texas of a journalist who published news stories about a border agent's public suicide and a car crash.

articles.title: Voice of America staffers sue, alleging Kari Lake put on propaganda
articles.summary: Voice of America staffers are suing Trump administration official Kari Lake, alleging she put pro-Trump propaganda on its airwaves. She has lost numerous rulings of late.

articles.title: Trump delays strikes on Iran's power plants for 5 days. And, ICE deploys to airports
articles.summary: Trump says he will deploy ICE agents to U.S. airports to help address delays. And, the president said he would delay strikes on Iranian power plants for five days.

articles.title: Trump says the U.S. is in talks with Iran to end the war, which Iran denies
articles.summary: Trump said the U.S. will postpone any strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days, even as Israel continued hitting Tehran and Iran warned it could retaliate across the Gulf.

articles.title: As D.C.'s cherry blossom trees near peak bloom, here's a guide to their history
articles.summary: The renowned trees along Washington, D.C's Tidal Basin were sent as a gift from Japan in 1912. Some of the original trees are still there.

articles.title: Ukrainians are growing more pessimistic about the prospects for peace, survey says
articles.summary: As the Iran war delays peace talks for Ukraine -- and global supplies of air defense missiles -- there are signs that fewer Ukrainians are willing to endure Russia's war for as long as necessary.

articles.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
articles.summary: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.

articles.title: NATO allies prepare for a Russian attack in the far North
articles.summary: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.

articles.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?
articles.summary: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.

articles.title: What a team of investigative reporters found out about Banksy's identity
articles.summary: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
